title: Rustic Middle-Eastern Ground Pork Fatteh
description: A unique and flavorful Middle-Eastern dish featuring ground pork, crunchy chickpeas, and sumac-spiced pita bread. Serve warm or at room temperature for a light and refreshing meal.
instructions: Bake pita bread slices for 5-7 minutes, or until crispy and lightly browned.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium-high heat. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add ground pork to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks. Cook until pork is no longer pink, about 5-7 minutes.
Add chickpeas, onion, parsley, mint, sumac, salt, and black pepper to the skillet. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, stirring frequently.
Stir in lemon juice and crushed red pepper flakes. Cook for 1 minute, or until the mixture is heated through.
To assemble fatteh, place a toasted pita bread slice on a plate or platter. Top with a spoonful of the pork and chickpea mixture, followed by a sprinkle of chopped cilantro.
Repeat the layers one more time, ending with a sprinkle of cilantro on top.
Serve warm or at room temperature, garnished with additional sumac and parsley if desired.
Repeat the process with the remaining pita bread and filling to serve the remaining guests.
Serve immediately, or refrigerate for up to 3 days and reheat before serving.
If refrigerating, reheat the fatteh in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 10-15 minutes, or until warmed through and crispy on top.
